chunk_summary: This section outlines therapeutic goals for managing uncomplicated moderate wasting through counselling, nutrient-dense home diets, and selective supplementation based on risk stratification, emphasizing WHO guidelines and energy needs during recovery.
learning_objectives: Understand the therapeutic goals for uncomplicated moderate wasting, including recovery and prevention of progression to SAM.
Learn WHO recommendations for nutrient-dense home diets and selective supplementation in routine and humanitarian contexts.
Identify risk factors for supplementation, such as MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, and severe social adversity.
Understand energy requirements during recovery and the role of supplements in meeting these needs.
key_facts: Primary therapeutic goals include recovery from wasting, neurodevelopment support, and infection prevention.
WHO prioritizes nutrient-dense home diets first, with supplementation for high-risk children (e.g., MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age <24 months).
In humanitarian contexts, supplementation may be considered for all children 6–59 months with moderate wasting.
Lipid-based nutrient supplements (LNS) are conditionally preferred by WHO; fortified blended foods are secondary options.
Supplements should contribute 40–60% of daily energy needs, with total energy needs estimated at 100–130 kcal/kg/day.
reference_quote: WHO anchors moderate wasting care in a nutrient-dense diet plus identification and treatment of medical and psychosocial drivers. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK601642/)
